internal_issues.description: WIN-017 cross-validation circularity preemption — period-luminosity calibration uses parallax anchor; spectroscopic-class independence not stated

suggested_patch.find: all methods agree, ruling out any global systematic that would affect geometric parallax alone. Spacecraft-based measurements
suggested_patch.replace: all methods agree, ruling out any global systematic that would affect geometric parallax alone. The cleanest independence test is the raw Gaia astrometric residuals — the annual sinusoidal position signature has amplitude that correlates with spectroscopic-class distance estimates (which set absolute magnitudes from spectral classification rather than from a parallax-calibrated period-luminosity relation), confirming that the 1/d pattern is in the geometry of the data and not an artifact of the photometric calibration chain. Spacecraft-based measurements

ASSIGN: ISS-1851 (WIN-021 escape hatch — rad/s² as acceleration defense) needs analyst EXP. Assign to analyst Mode 3 (defense neutralization). Preemptive text suggested in WIN-021.c3 digest: 'Add ~2 sentences preempting the acceleration interpretation: even if rad/s² were intentional, VLBI measures the precession rate as effectively constant on human timescales — a specific angular-acceleration value would require a changing precession rate not observed in historical data or modern geodesy.'
